revision.text: [[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]]
[[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{Commons cat|Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)}}

{{神社 |名称=Ogura Shrine ({{ill|小倉神社}}) |画像=[[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地=83 Enmyōji Torii-mae, [[Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|{{ill|大山崎町}}]], [[Otokuni District, Kyoto|{{ill|乙訓郡}}]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|京都府}}]] |緯度度=34 |緯度分=54 |緯度秒=34.4 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135 |経度分=40 |経度秒=38.1 |E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=[[Takemikazuchi|{{ill|武甕槌神}}]]
[[Futsunushi|{{ill|経津主神}}]]
[[Amenokoyane|{{ill|天児屋命}}]]
[[Himegami|{{ill|比売大神}}]] |社格={{ill|式内社}}（大）
旧{{ill|郷社}} |創建=養老2年（718） |本殿=三間社流造 |例祭=May 3 |地図=Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Ogura Shrine''' ({{ill|小倉神社}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at 83 Enmyōji Torii-mae in Ōyamazaki, Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. == Enshrined deities == * [[Takemikazuchi|{{ill|武甕槌神}}]] * [[Futsunushi|{{ill|経津主神}}]] * [[Amenokoyane|{{ill|天児屋命}}]] * [[Himegami|{{ill|比売大神}}]] == History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded in Yōrō 2 (718). When the capital was moved to Heian, it was invoked to ward off evil at the kimon (demon gate) of the imperial palace, and in Kajō 3 (850) it was granted the Senior First Rank (正一位), although this event is not recorded in the *Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku*. In the *Engishiki* register of shrines (延喜式神名帳), it appears as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ō Ogura Shrine – monthly and Niiname rites.” During the Azuchi–Momoyama period, at the Battle of Yamazaki, Toyotomi Hideyoshi dispatched his retainers Katagiri Katsumoto and Wakisaka Yasuji to pray here for victory. After the battle, he donated three thousand koku of rice annually in thanksgiving. In the Edo period, the Tokugawa shogunate granted over twenty chō of mountain land, and by the Meiji Restoration the former precincts covered more than sixty thousand tsubo. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a village shrine (村社); in Meiji 16 (1883), it was promoted to a county shrine (郷社). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Detached haiden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * From [[Nishiyama-tennozan Station]] on the Hankyu Kyoto Main Line, about a 15-minute walk west. * From the Hankyu Bus “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae” stop, about a 6-minute walk. == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine == See also == {{Commonscat}} * [[List of Engishiki shrines in Yamashiro Province|{{ill|山城国の式内社一覧}}]] * [[Ogura Shrine (Uji)|{{ill|巨椋神社}}]] – a shrine in Uji, Kyoto Prefecture == External links == *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくらしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大山崎町の歴史]] [[Category:大山崎町の建築物]] [[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{Commons cat|Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)}}

{{神社 |名称=Ogura Shrine ({{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|小倉神社}}) |画像=[[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地=83 Enmyōji Torii-mae, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 54 | 緯度秒 = 34.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 38.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}（{{ill|Ōsha|ja|大社}}）
former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建={{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老}} 2 (718) |本殿=three-bay (三間社) {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |例祭=May 3 | 地図 = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Entrance}}]] '''Ogura Shrine''' ({{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|小倉神社}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老}} 2 (718). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from [[Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station]] on the [[Hankyu Kyoto Main Line]]. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{Commons cat|Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)}}

In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[:en:Katagiri_Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[:en:Wakisaka_Yasuharu|Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from [[Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station]] on the [[Hankyu Kyoto Main Line]]. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{Commons cat|Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)}}

In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[:en:Katagiri_Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[:en:Wakisaka_Yasuharu|Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from [[:en:Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line]]. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{Commons cat|Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)}}

former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建={{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老}} 2 (718) |本殿=three-bay (三間社) {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |例祭=May 3 | 地図 = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Entrance}}]] {{nihongo|'''Ogura Shrine'''|小倉神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[:en:Katagiri_Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[:en:Wakisaka_Yasuharu|Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[:en:Katagiri_Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[:en:Wakisaka_Yasuharu|Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta revision.comment: I realize that doing a recent edit on these ones is better than all of that other crap of manual cat a lot stuff
revision.text:
{{{afc comment|1=wikidata [[d:Q63891076]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:12, 3 August 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building|image=Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg |名称=Ogura Shrine ({{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|小倉神社}}) |画像=[[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地=83 Enmyōji Torii-mae, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 54 | 緯度秒 = 34.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 38.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}（{{ill|Ōsha|ja|大社}}）
former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建={{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老}} 2 (718) |本殿=three-bay (三間社) {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |例祭=May 3 | 地図 = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Entrance}}]] {{nihongo|'''Ogura Shrine'''|小倉神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[:en:Katagiri_Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[:en:Wakisaka_Yasuharu|Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from [[:en:Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line]]. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]][[Category:Municipal History of Ōyamazaki]][[Category:Buildings in Oyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]][[Category:Municipal History of Ōyamazaki]][[Category:Buildings in Oyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]][[Category:Municipal History of Ōyamazaki]][[Category:Buildings in Oyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]][[Category:Municipal History of Ōyamazaki]][[Category:Buildings in Oyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[:en:Katagiri_Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[:en:Wakisaka_Yasuharu|Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from [[:en:Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line]]. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]][[Category:Municipal History of revision.comment: Text replacement - "qid=" to "WD="
revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{{afc comment|1=wikidata [[d:Q63891076]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:12, 3 August 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building|image=Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg |名称=Ogura Shrine ({{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|小倉神社}}) |画像=[[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地=83 Enmyōji Torii-mae, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 54 | 緯度秒 = 34.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 38.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}（{{ill|Ōsha|ja|大社}}）
former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建={{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老}} 2 (718) |本殿=three-bay (三間社) {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |例祭=May 3 | 地図 = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Entrance}}]] {{nihongo|'''Ogura Shrine'''|小倉神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[:en:Katagiri_Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[:en:Wakisaka_Yasuharu|Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from [[:en:Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line]]. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]][[Category:Municipal History of Ōyamazaki]][[Category:Buildings in Oyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]][[Category:Municipal History of It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[:en:Katagiri_Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[:en:Wakisaka_Yasuharu|Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta [[sn:commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{{afc comment|1=wikidata [[da:d:Q63891076]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:12, 3 August 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building|image=Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg |名称=Ogura Shrine ({{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|小倉神社}}) |画像=[[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地=83 Enmyōji Torii-mae, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 54 | 緯度秒 = 34.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 38.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}（{{ill|Ōsha|ja|大社}}）
former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建={{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老}} 2 (718) |本殿=three-bay (三間社) {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |例祭=May 3 | 地図 = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Entrance}}]] {{nihongo|'''Ogura Shrine'''|小倉神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[:en:Katagiri_Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[:en:Wakisaka_Yasuharu|Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from [[:en:Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line]]. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]][[Category:Municipal History of former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建={{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老}} 2 (718) |本殿=three-bay (三間社) {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |例祭=May 3 | 地図 = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Entrance}}]] {{nihongo|'''Ogura Shrine'''|小倉神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[:en:Katagiri_Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[:en:Wakisaka_Yasuharu|Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from [[:en:Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line]]. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]][[Category:Municipal History of revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{{afc comment|1=wikidata [[da:d:Q63891076]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:12, 3 August 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building|image=Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg |名称=Ogura Shrine ({{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|小倉神社}}) |画像=[[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地=83 Enmyōji Torii-mae, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 54 | 緯度秒 = 34.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 38.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}（{{ill|Ōsha|ja|大社}}）
former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建={{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老}} 2 (718) |本殿=three-bay (三間社) {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |例祭=May 3 | 地図 = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Entrance}}]] {{nihongo|'''Ogura Shrine'''|小倉神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[:en:Katagiri_Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[:en:Wakisaka_Yasuharu|Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from [[:en:Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line]]. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]][[Category:Municipal History of Ōyamazaki]][[Category:Buildings in Oyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]][[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]][[Category:Municipal History of [[commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] [[commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{{afc comment|1=wikidata [[da:d:Q63891076]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:12, 3 August 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building|image=Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg |名称=Ogura Shrine ({{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|小倉神社}}) |画像=[[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地=83 Enmyōji Torii-mae, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 54 | 緯度秒 = 34.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 38.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}（{{ill|Ōsha|ja|大社}}）
former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建={{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老}} 2 (718) |本殿=three-bay (三間社) {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |例祭=May 3 | 地図 = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Entrance}}]] {{nihongo|'''Ogura Shrine'''|小倉神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers [[:en:Katagiri_Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] and [[:en:Wakisaka_Yasuharu|Wakisaka Yasuharu]] to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from [[:en:Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line]]. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{Commons cat|Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q63891076}} [[Category:Buildings in Oyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōyamazaki]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{{afc comment|1=wikidata [[da:d:Q63891076]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:12, 3 August 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building|image=Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg |名称=Ogura Shrine ({{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|小倉神社}}) |画像=[[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地=83 Enmyōji Torii-mae, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 54 | 緯度秒 = 34.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 38.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}（{{ill|Ōsha|ja|大社}}）
former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建={{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老}} 2 (718) |本殿=three-bay (三間社) {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |例祭=May 3 | 地図 = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Entrance}}]] {{nihongo|'''Ogura Shrine'''|小倉神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers {{ill|Katagiri_Katsumoto|en|Katagiri_Katsumoto|WD=Q1882775|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto}} and {{ill|Wakisaka_Yasuharu|en|Wakisaka_Yasuharu|WD=Q1194978|lt=Wakisaka Yasuharu}} to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from {{ill|Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|en|Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|WD=Q650349|lt=Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station}} on the {{ill|Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|en|Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|WD=Q2053737|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}}. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{Commons cat|Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q63891076}} [[Category:Buildings in Oyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōyamazaki]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from {{ill|Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|en|Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|WD=Q650349|lt=Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station}} on the {{ill|Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|en|Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|WD=Q2053737|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}}. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{Commons cat|Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q63891076}} [[Category:Buildings in Oyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōyamazaki]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] { {{infobox religious building|image=Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg |名称=Ogura Shrine ({{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|小倉神社}}) |画像=[[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地=83 Enmyōji Torii-mae, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 54 | 緯度秒 = 34.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 38.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}（{{ill|Ōsha|ja|大社}}）
former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建={{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老}} 2 (718) |本殿=three-bay (三間社) {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |例祭=May 3 | 地図 = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Entrance}}]] {{nihongo|'''Ogura Shrine'''|小倉神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers {{ill|Katagiri_Katsumoto|en|Katagiri_Katsumoto|WD=Q1882775|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto}} and {{ill|Wakisaka_Yasuharu|en|Wakisaka_Yasuharu|WD=Q1194978|lt=Wakisaka Yasuharu}} to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from {{ill|Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|en|Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|WD=Q650349|lt=Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station}} on the {{ill|Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|en|Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|WD=Q2053737|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}}. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{Commons cat|Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q63891076}} [[Category:Buildings in Oyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōyamazaki]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers {{ill|Katagiri_Katsumoto|en|Katagiri_Katsumoto|WD=Q1882775|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} and {{ill|Wakisaka_Yasuharu|en|Wakisaka_Yasuharu|WD=Q1194978|lt=Wakisaka Yasuharu|1=Wakisaka Yasuharu}} to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from {{ill|Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|en|Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|WD=Q650349|lt=Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station|1=Nishiyama Tennozan Station}} on the {{ill|Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|en|Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|WD=Q2053737|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}}. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also [[co:commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)]][[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] { {{infobox religious building|image=Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg |名称=Ogura Shrine ({{ill|Ogura Shrine|ja|小倉神社}}) |画像=[[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地=83 Enmyōji Torii-mae, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 54 | 緯度秒 = 34.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 38.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}（{{ill|Ōsha|ja|大社}}）
former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建={{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老}} 2 (718) |本殿=three-bay (三間社) {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |例祭=May 3 | 地図 = Japan Kyoto }} [[File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Entrance}}]] {{nihongo|'''Ogura Shrine'''|小倉神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|ja|大山崎町}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. == Enshrined deities == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}} == History == According to shrine tradition, the foundation dates to {{ill|Yōrō (era)|ja|養老|lt=Yōrō}} 2 ([[718]]). At the transfer to Heian-kyō, prayers were offered here to guard the imperial palace’s {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門}} (“demon gate”). It is said the shrine received the Senior First Rank in Kajō 3 (850), though this is not recorded in {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} register ({{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}) it is listed as “Yamashiro Province, Otokuni District: Ogura Shrine (major), for the monthly and Niiname rites.” During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|ja|山崎の戦い}}, [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] sent retainers {{ill|Katagiri_Katsumoto|en|Katagiri_Katsumoto|WD=Q1882775|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} and {{ill|Wakisaka_Yasuharu|en|Wakisaka_Yasuharu|WD=Q1194978|lt=Wakisaka Yasuharu|1=Wakisaka Yasuharu}} to pray for victory; thereafter a yearly offering of 3,000 ''hyō'' of rice was made. In the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted over twenty ''chō'' of mountain land, and by the [[Meiji Restoration]] the former precincts totaled over 60,000 ''tsubo''. Designated a village shrine (村社) in Meiji 6 (1873), it was promoted to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in Meiji 16 (1883). == Precincts == File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Katsuhaiden.jpg|Katsu-haiden (split/de­tached worship hall) File:Oguta Jinja Oyamazaki Shinboku.jpg|Sacred tree (御神木) File:Oguta_Jinja_Oyamazaki_Torii.jpg|First torii == Access == * About 15 minutes’ walk west from {{ill|Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|en|Nishiyama_Tennozan_Station|WD=Q650349|lt=Nishiyama-Tennōzan Station|1=Nishiyama Tennozan Station}} on the {{ill|Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|en|Hankyu_Kyoto_Main_Line|WD=Q2053737|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}}. * About 6 minutes’ walk from the Hankyu Bus stop “Daini Ōyamazaki Elementary School-mae.” == References == {{参照方法|date=2019年5月|section=1}} * Ogura Shrine Chronicle (由緒書) * Hiroshi Tanke, “Ogura-san Faith and the Aristocratic Culture Surrounding It,” Ogura Shrine. == See also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ogura Shrine (Uji)|ja|巨椋神社}} – a shrine in [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]], Kyoto Prefecture. == External links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/017003.html Ogura Shrine] – (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto & Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogura Jinja}} [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社_(大山崎町)]] [[ja:小倉神社 (大山崎町)]] {{Commons cat|Ogura-jinja (Oyamazaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q63891076}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Oyamazaki, Kyoto]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōyamazaki]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
